title: Correcting a Misprepared Balance Sheet
event: Ingle Co.'s draft balance sheet reports total assets of $500,000, but the figure includes $20,000 of treasury stock listed among assets, and the draft omits $15,000 of declared but unpaid dividends from liabilities. Compute corrected total assets and identify the liability fix.

computationPaths.narration: Treasury stock is the company's own shares — you cannot own a piece of yourself as an asset. It's contra-equity. Declared dividends are a real, legal liability.

memorize.body: Treasury stock is NEVER an asset — it reduces stockholders' equity. The single most common balance-sheet correction on exams.
Declared dividends become a liability at DECLARATION, not at payment.

questions.prompt: Draft assets $500,000 including $20,000 of treasury stock. Corrected total assets = ?

distractors.feedback: Treasury stock must come OUT of assets — it's contra-equity: 480,000.
You also removed the dividends payable from ASSETS. That error lives on the liability side — it doesn't touch assets.
